hero_subtitle: SFTY helps compliance teams rescreen customers and entities against updated sanctions lists by connecting provider outputs into structured workflows with traceable audit evidence and case management.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to keep up when updated sanctions lists require repeated rescreening across separate tools. This creates manual workloads and gaps in audit evidence that complicate case tracking and regulatory review readiness.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects updated sanctions data, provider alerts, case management and audit evidence into one continuous workflow so compliance teams can efficiently triage, review and document rescreening outcomes.
workflow_title: From sanctions alerts to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY routes updated sanctions screening alerts through review, case creation, decision logging and evidence preservation within a controlled AML compliance workflow.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond disconnected alerts. One audit-ready layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY turns fragmented sanctions alerts into connected cases with review history and evidence, enabling clearer operational control and audit-ready compliance for sanctions rescreening workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs managing updated sanctions screening with controlled provider orchestration, defensible evidence, bank due diligence and enterprise deployment standards.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for sanctions compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects sanctions screening outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions and evidence to support VASP compliance teams managing AML sanctions operations and updated screening requirements.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle sanctions rescreening workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY automates rescreening by routing updated sanctions lists through provider outputs, triggering alerts, opening cases, escalating reviews and tracking decision logs for compliance control.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve sanctions screening evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-ready evidence by recording alert data, review actions and decision history within cases, supporting traceability for sanctions rescreening and internal or regulator review.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing screening providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates provider outputs from various sanctions screening vendors, connecting alerts and results into one operational layer without disrupting existing compliance tools.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions screening and monitoring tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace sanctions or screening tools but connects and structures their outputs into end-to-end sanctions compliance workflows and evidence management.
final_title: Structure sanctions rescreening into controlled cases
final_description: Review how SFTY connects updated sanctions alerts into workflows, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence for VASP compliance teams.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
